headlines.title: A third billionaire testifies in OpenAI trial: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella
headlines.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella took the witness stand Monday in a trial about control of the artificial intelligence startup OpenAI, making him the third tech billionaire to testify in the closely watched lawsuit in as many weeks. Nadella’s testimony follows earlier appearances by Elon Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX and a co-founder of OpenAI, and Greg Brockman, the president and co-founder of OpenAI. A fourth billionaire, OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever, took the witness stand later Monday and said his stake in OpenAI’s for-profit arm is worth about $7 billion. And a fifth billionaire, OpenAI co-founder and CEO Sam Altman, is scheduled to testify later in the week. The lawsuit centers around Musk’s claims that OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT, has strayed from its original mission as a nonprofit research center. Musk alleges that Altman and Brockman are enriching themselves at the expense of what he says was supposed to remain a charity. Artificial intelligence 7 hours ago Artificial intelligence May 7 Artificial intelligence May 6 Altman and Brockman say that OpenAI is still controlled by a nonprofit foundation, despite having a for-profit arm with outside investors. They say Musk is now harassing them as competitors via the suit after he started his own AI company, xAI, now part of SpaceX. They also say Musk agreed with them about the need to start a for-profit arm but wanted to be the one to control it. Microsoft is a co-defendant in the case through its partnership with OpenAI. Musk alleges that Microsoft was a bad influence on Altman and Brockman, encouraging them to violate their duty to OpenAI’s nonprofit mission by signing lucrative deals in which OpenAI obtained cloud computing services. In response, Microsoft has said it wasn’t aware of any conditions that Musk, as a donor to OpenAI, might have placed on his charitable contributions. The company says it therefore didn’t intend to encourage Altman and Brockman to violate their duty. On the witness stand, Nadella testified that OpenAI retained its independence even as the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership deepened over time. “OpenAI had all the rights and resources they always had,” he said. In 2019, Microsoft agreed to invest $1 billion in OpenAI’s for-profit arm and provide cloud computing services in exchange for rights to a share of profits and an exclusive license to commercialize one of OpenAI’s models for a year. Microsoft added another $2 billion in 2021 and another $10 billion in 2023, after OpenAI had released ChatGPT. Musk complained publicly in 2020 that OpenAI seemed to have been captured. “This does seem like the opposite of open. OpenAI is essentially captured by Microsoft,” Musk wrote on Twitter, now X, in September that year. The next month, according to notes from an October 2020 meeting introduced as pre-trial evidence, Nadella discussed Musk’s perspective on “closed openai” and said it was worth thinking through, after not wanting “to get caught up in something,” according to notes from the October 2020 meeting introduced in the lawsuit pre-trial. U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers cited the notes as one piece of “considerable evidence” for sending the accusation against Microsoft to trial. The closeness between Microsoft and OpenAI has flared up at other points. In 2023, when Altman was briefly ousted as OpenAI CEO, Nadella on a podcast emphasized how tight they had become. “We are below them, above them, around them,” Nadella said on the podcast, “On with Kara Swisher.” On the witness stand last week, Musk adviser and former OpenAI board member Shivon Zilis quoted that podcast interview as a moment when the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership had given her pause. Nadella testified Monday that his statement on the podcast was in the context of Microsoft customers fearing that OpenAI might disappear during the November 2023 crisis at the company. “It goes back to me trying to communicate to customers that they can rely on Microsoft,” he said. “It had nothing to do with control.” Musk sued in 2024, saying his past donations to OpenAI totaling $38 million gave him a special interest in seeing that it remained a nonprofit research center. He had also founded xAI a year earlier. Nadella became Microsoft’s CEO in 2014, succeeding Steve Ballmer. He has a net worth of $1.3 billion, according to Forbes magazine. Evidence in the trial is scheduled to wrap up Wednesday, with closing statements to the nine-member jury expected Thursday.

headlines.title: Santa Clara County sues Meta over misleading ads
headlines.description: Santa Clara County filed a civil lawsuit Monday against Meta, accusing the social media company of targeting users with misleading and fraudulent advertisements. The lawsuit, filed on behalf of all California residents, alleges the Menlo Park-based company tolerated and profited from scam ads on its platforms. According to the complaint, Meta may have earned as much as $7 billion annually from advertisements that showed “clear signs of being fraudulent.” The county also claims the company blocked or limited efforts to reduce scams if those measures affected revenue. Santa Clara County is seeking restitution and civil damages. Meta provided the following statement on Monday: “This claim relies on Reuters reporting that distorts our motives and ignores the full range of actions we take to combat scams every day. We aggressively fight scams on and off our platforms because they’re not good for us or the people and businesses that rely on our services. We removed over 159 million scam ads last year alone, launched new tools to protect people, and partnered with law enforcement around the globe to disrupt these criminals. We will fight this lawsuit.”

headlines.title: 2 charged for allegedly organizing 2025 sideshow during IShowSpeed visit
headlines.description: Two men are facing misdemeanor charges for allegedly organizing a 2025 sideshow that took over West Grand Avenue in Oakland and coincided with a visit by internet celebrity IShowSpeed. Alameda County District Attorney Ursula Jones Dickson announced Monday that Axel Cisnerosmentos and Aron Anaya have been charged with organizing and facilitating a sideshow and with aiding and abetting a speed contest. Both allegedly organized the sideshow during IShowSpeed’s visit to Oakland on Nov. 25. The YouTube sensation, who has nearly 54 million followers on that platform, stopped by Hyphy Burger on West Grand near Market Street in West Oakland as part of his well-publicized and live-streamed “Speed Does America” tour. While he was at the restaurant, hundreds of people showed up trying to capture his attention, which prompted IShowSpeed and his team to get onto the rooftop in an effort to escape a swarm of boisterous fans. The intersection near the restaurant was soon filled with fireworks, lowrider cars and fans shouting “Speed!” Several cars eventually began doing donuts at the intersection, with people chasing after them. IShowSpeed was visibly bewildered by the sideshow. “Why are they running to the car … are they stupid?” he said. “I can’t watch this.” The sideshow prompted him to leave, which was another difficult task in itself. IShowSpeed wiggled his way out of the restaurant between hordes of fans begging for a photo with him. “Sideshows are not harmless gatherings,” Jones Dickson said in a news release Monday. “These illegal events routinely cause vandalism, reckless driving, injuries, property damage, and pose significant threats to public safety. Individuals who organize, promote, and facilitate these activities will be held accountable.” Cisnerosmentos and Anaya are scheduled to appear for arraignment on May 27 at the Wiley Manuel Courthouse in Oakland. A link to IShowSpeed’s livestream in Oakland can be found here. San Francisco Sep 25, 2025 synced in Sep 26, 2025 In the news: OPD's new pursuit policy, IShowSpeed takes over San Francisco, Bay Area bachelorette

headlines.title: Caltrans finishes second of three 19th Avenue closures early in San Francisco
headlines.description: Caltrans completed its second weekend closure along 19th Avenue several hours ahead of schedule, reopening lanes early Monday morning. Crews finished repaving work along three southbound lanes at 19th Avenue and Lincoln Way around 1:30 a.m., about three and a half hours earlier than expected. The work is part of an ongoing repaving project stretching southbound from Lincoln Way to Sloat Boulevard. This marks the second of three planned weekend closures. Caltrans is now preparing for the third and final closure, scheduled for Memorial Day weekend. During that time, 19th Avenue will be closed in both directions between Sloat and Holloway avenues, near Stonestown and San Francisco State University. Officials said the weekend closures are designed to reduce overall disruption, shortening the project timeline from about 40 days of weekday work to 10 days of concentrated construction. The final closure is set to begin Friday, May 22 at 7 a.m. and continue through Monday, May 25 at 10 a.m. San Francisco May 8 San Francisco Apr 27 Highway 1 (19th Avenue) in San Francisco reopens early after weekend roadwork

headlines.title: Lyft passenger killed on I-80 in Vallejo after dispute with driver
headlines.description: A 50-year-old Vallejo man was killed Saturday when he got out a Lyft ride-hailing vehicle on Interstate Highway 80 after a dispute with the driver, the California Highway Patrol said. The Lyft driver, described as a Middle Eastern man in his 30s from Sacramento, told the highway patrol he was taking the man home from a bar in Benecia at about 1 a.m. In Vallejo, the driver said, the passenger began to verbally and physically assault him on eastbound I-80 east of Tennessee Street. When the driver stopped and got out of his car to get away, the passenger began to chase him on the right shoulder toward Redwood Parkway, the CHP said. The passenger was struck in the far-right lane by a black Audi sedan driven by a Vallejo woman in her early 20s, according to the highway patrol. “The Audi driver did not see the pedestrian until it was too late, and the Lyft driver only heard the crash,” the CHP reported.

headlines.title: Man arrested after stabbing at Redwood City grocery store
headlines.description: A man was arrested after a stabbing at a Redwood City grocery store Saturday night, police said. Officers responded around 10:59 p.m. to reports of an altercation at a Safeway store on El Camino Real. Witnesses at the scene pointed out the suspect, who was detained without incident. Police said an adult male victim suffered a stab wound to the abdomen and additional minor injuries. He was treated at the scene by Redwood City Fire Department personnel. Investigators said the victim was in the self-checkout area with an adult woman when the suspect approached and began attacking him with a knife without apparent provocation. The woman used a shopping cart to try to stop the assault. During the incident, the male victim fired a gun once, striking a computer monitor inside the store. No one was injured by the gunfire. The suspect fled into the parking lot but was quickly detained by officers. Police identified the suspect as 31-year-old Isidro Zamora-Cisneros of Redwood City. He was booked into the San Mateo County Maguire Correctional Facility on suspicion of attempted murder and drug-related charges. Investigators said it is not yet known whether the suspect and victims knew each other. Police do not believe there are any additional suspects and said there is no ongoing threat to the public. Anyone with information related to this case is encouraged to contact the Redwood City Police Department’s non-emergency line at (650) 780-7100.

headlines.title: OpenAI trial: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella wraps up testimony
headlines.description: What to Know Elon Musk filed a $134 billion lawsuit against Sam Altman and his top lieutenant, Greg Brockman, along with Microsoft, over its investments in OpenAI, in 2024. Musk claims OpenAI violated its original mission to operate as a nonprofit focused on developing artificial intelligence for the benefit of humanity. OpenAI has brushed off Musk’s allegations as a case of sour grapes aimed at undercutting its rapid growth and bolstering Musk’s own xAI, which he launched in 2023 as a competitor. The nine-person jury was selected on April 27. The trial is being held in Oakland federal court and scheduled to continue through late May. Closing arguments are expected to start Thursday, May 14. Editor’s note: This live blog has ended and is no longer being updated. NBC Bay Area’s Damian Trujillo is at the federal courthouse in Oakland and providing live updates below.
headlines.source: Damian Trujillo, Kristofer Noceda and Joel Soria

headlines.title: Bay Area lawmakers push bill to cap concert ticket resale prices
headlines.description: Bay Area lawmakers are working on new legislation aimed at lowering the cost of concert tickets and cracking down on scalping. Assemblymember Matt Haney announced the “California Fans First Act” on Monday, a proposal that would cap ticket resale prices at 10% above face value. Supporters said the measure would target large-scale ticket speculators while still allowing fan-to-fan resale. The goal is to improve fairness and transparency in the ticket-buying process. Haney promoted the bill outside San Francisco’s Castro Theatre, calling for more affordable access to live events. Another bill under consideration would ban sellers from advertising or selling “speculative” tickets they do not own or have a license to sell. The push comes after a jury earlier this month ruled that Live Nation operated an illegal monopoly in the primary ticket market and overcharged some concertgoers.

headlines.title: 4 injured after boat hits pier at Antioch Marina
headlines.description: Four people were injured following a boat incident at Antioch Marina on Sunday, according to the Contra Costa County Fire Department. Officials said a boat with occupants hit poles of a pier at around 6:40 p.m. As a result, authorities said four people suffered injuries that ranged from minor to moderate. The Contra Costa County Fire Department added there were no reported injuries from any restaurant or establishment in the area.

headlines.title: Santa Clara County resident was on the cruise with Andes hantavirus outbreak
headlines.description: A Santa Clara County resident was one of the Californians aboard the cruise ship affected by the Andes hantavirus outbreak, according to public health authorities. The County of Santa Clara Public Health Department announced they are monitoring and evaluating a resident who was exposed to the Andes hantavirus while a passenger on the MV Hondius cruise ship. The resident was one of the Californians who returned home, according to officials. The health department assured there is no known risk to the public at this time. According to authorities, hantaviruses are “zoonotic viruses” that infect rodents and are occasionally transmitted to humans – the Andes virus is part of this family. Officials said people usually get infected through contact with “infected rodents or their urine, droppings, or saliva”. Andes hantavirus can cause limited person-to-person transmission only with close and prolonged contact, which can be usually among household members or intimate partners. Health May 9 Health May 8 California May 7 Some passengers on the cruise with hantavirus outbreak are back in California

headlines.title: Man arrested after stealing DoorDash driver's car in Santa Rosa
headlines.description: A man was arrested after allegedly stealing a DoorDash driver’s car and damaging several patrol vehicles during a pursuit in Santa Rosa on Saturday, according to the police department. Police said officers responded to a reported vehicle theft in the area of 515 4th Street at around 8:50 p.m. The victim was a DoorDash driver, who had briefly exited the vehicle while leaving it running to deliver food, according to authorities. At around 10 p.m., the stolen vehicle triggered a Flock alert, and police said officers found it. Officials said they attempted a traffic stop but the driver, identified as Sergio Villalobos-Espinoza, drove away, initiating a pursuit. During the incident, police said the suspect drove aggressively and rammed two marked police vehicles. The pursuit ended after officers blocked Villalobos-Espinoza’s escape, and he was taken into custody, according to authorities. The police department said nobody was injured, but several patrol vehicles sustained moderate damage. According to authorities, Villalobos-Espinoza was taken to a hospital for medical clearance and later booked on suspicion of vehicle theft, felony evading, assault with a deadly weapon on a peace officer and enhanced penalty for repeat theft offender. Police added the suspect has at least 13 prior arrests for vehicle thefts in Sonoma and Mendocino counties.

headlines.title: Oakland average home values trending down, according to Zillow report
headlines.description: New numbers from Zillow show that average home values in Oakland have been going down. According to the Zillow Home Values Index, which factors in data from all types of homes, the average home value in Oakland is $716,248 as of March 31 of this year. Factoring in inflation, that is down 11.4% from the same time last year and 28.1% from 2019. Analysis of the data by the San Francisco Chronicle also found that Oakland was tied with Cape Coral, Florida, as the U.S. cities with 100,000 or more residents that saw the greatest percent decrease in home value since 2025. Local real estate agents tell NBC Bay Area that, while they’ve seen a cooling in the market for some areas in Oakland, when it comes to in-demand neighborhoods, conditions are still competitive for buyers. “In certain neighborhoods like Crocker Highlands here, most homes will sell in after, say two weekends of open houses, and they’ll get multiple offers and go 20% to 40%, sometimes more than that over the list price,” said Ben Kahn, a real estate agent who specializes in the Oakland, Berkeley, and Piedmont markets. Sunday, Kahn was hosting an open house for a Tudor home on Hubert Road. The four-bed, three-bath home was built in 1929 and is listed for $1.79 Million, but Kahn thinks it’s likely the home could sell for more than $2 Million. “There’s a lot of competition for these houses,” he noted, “and there’s not enough inventory to supply the demand.” Still, Kahn said, there are opportunities for buyers who can afford to buy in Oakland to purchase homes with more space or more unique details than they might find in other Bay Area cities. Real estate agent Sierra Amato, who represents buyers and sellers around the Bay Area, including many in Oakland, agreed that the market is still competitive all around in Oakland. “We have a place where a lot of sellers who bought during COVID are struggling to recoup what they paid, and this is creating a really challenging situation,” she explained, adding that buyers are also still feeling the pain of unaffordable options. “For the average person, sellers and buyers, it is really a struggle, I think, it is playing out in the market,” she continued, adding that she’s seeing a growing number of sellers insisting on selling at a certain amount to break even, while buyers are asking for discounts and costs covered to make the purchase more affordable. Regarding Zillow’s recent numbers for Oakland, Amato said she is waiting to see the updated numbers in a few months, because she has personally seen sales picking up this spring. Another factor that could drive up Oakland’s home values is the growing AI boom in San Francisco, which has driven up home sales and rents. “I also think that we’ll start to see spillover from San Francisco because the bubble there is so great, so people can’t afford it,” Amato continued.
